title: Anchor Infrastructure Requirements
summary: Defines Anchor's eight infrastructure layers (INF-001 to INF-008), evaluates agent-runtime isolation options, and specifies the MVP deployment baseline and data-layer schema contracts.
scope: integration layer (Telegram, Google Calendar, LLM provider, STT, email)
notes: Classified SPEC over PRD/DOC: content is dominated by numbered technical constraints (INF-001..INF-008), a data-layer schema contract enforced by a CHECK constraint, named foreign-key/source_kind contracts, and retrieval capability requirements the system must honor — implementation contracts, not user-facing product requirements. The trade-off analysis of runtime Options A/B/C ends in an explicit MVP recommendation but the doc states 'It does not commit to a final stack' and defers binding choices to ADRs 0001-0004, so it is not an ADR (no locked decision, no Accepted status). cross_refs are ADR numbers referenced in the 'ADR candidates' section; the file contains no markdown links or explicit file paths.
